questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following mixtures can be separated by filtration?

options.label: A
options.text: a mixture of oil and water

options.label: B
options.text: a mixture of ethanol and water

options.label: C
options.text: a mixture of silver chloride and water

options.label: D
options.text: a mixture of sodium chloride and water

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following gases can cause acid rain?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Solid W dissolves in water to form an alkaline solution. What is W?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The table below shows the molar masses of four fertilisers. Which of the following fertilisers has the highest percentage by mass of nitrogen?
FertiliserMolar mass / g
\( (NH_{2})_{2}CO \)60.0
\( NH_{4}NO_{3} \)80.0
\( NaNO_{3} \)85.0
\( (NH_{4})_{2}SO_{4} \)132.1
(Relative atomic mass : N = 14.0)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following experimental set-up :
[IMG:0.jpg]

What can be observed after the circuit is closed for a period of time?

options.label: A
options.text: A purple patch and a blue patch migrate towards P.

options.label: B
options.text: A purple patch and a blue patch migrate towards Q.

options.label: C
options.text: A purple patch migrates towards P while a blue patch migrates towards Q.

options.label: D
options.text: A purple patch migrates towards Q while a blue patch migrates towards P.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Metal X is less reactive than silver. 2.21 g of an oxide of X is heated strongly until no further reaction. The mass of the solid remaining is 1.97 g. What is the chemical formula of this oxide?

(Relative atomic masses : O = 16.0, X = 197.0)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning aqueous ammonia is INCORRECT?

options.label: A
options.text: Aqueous ammonia is a weak alkali.

options.label: C
options.text: Aqueous ammonia can be used to distinguish between Pb(NO_{3})_{2}(aq) and Al(NO_{3})_{3}(aq).

options.label: D
options.text: When aqueous ammonia is added to  \( CuSO_{4}(aq) \)  until in excess, a deep blue solution is formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following is endothermic?

options.label: A
options.text: dilution of concentrated sulphuric acid with water

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Which of the following equations can represent a possible reaction for the cracking of  \( C_{16}H_{34} \)  to give propene and other organic products?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text: Petroleum can be separated into different fractions by fractional distillation in a fractionating tower. Fraction U is obtained from the upper part of the fractionating tower and fraction L is obtained from the lower part of the fractionating tower. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Fraction U has a higher boiling range and a higher viscosity than fraction L.

options.label: B
options.text: Fraction U has a higher boiling range and a lower viscosity than fraction L.

options.label: C
options.text: Fraction U has a lower boiling range and a higher viscosity than fraction L.

options.label: D
options.text: Fraction U has a lower boiling range and a lower viscosity than fraction L.

questions.question_text:

[IMG:0.jpg] [IMG:1.jpg]
[IMG:2.jpg]

Which of the following correctly shows the descending order of reactivity of the three metals P, Q and R?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: When 40 cm³ of 0.5 M hydrochloric acid is mixed with 40 cm³ of 0.5 M sodium hydroxide solution, there is a temperature rise of  \( \Delta T \) . What would be the temperature rise if 60 cm³ of 1.0 M hydrochloric acid is mixed with 60 cm³ of 0.5 M sodium hydroxide solution?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following combinations concerning the electrolysis of copper(II) sulphate solution is correct?

Anode    |    Cathode    |    Observation

options.label: A
options.text: graphite    |    graphite    |    A reddish brown solid is deposited on the anode

options.label: B
options.text: graphite    |    copper    |    The mass of the cathode decreases

options.label: C
options.text: copper    |    copper    |    The blue colour of the solution fades out

options.label: D
options.text: copper    |    graphite    |    The mass of the anode decreases

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
0.729 g of magnesium ribbon reacts with 50.0 cm \( ^{3} \)  of 0.50 M sulphuric acid. What is the mass of magnesium ribbon remained after the reaction has completed?

(Relative atomic mass : Mg = 24.3)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Argon is a gas at room temperature and pressure because

options.label: A
options.text: the outermost electron shell of an argon atom has an octet structure.

options.label: B
options.text: the attractive force between argon atoms is weak.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction :
\( Cl_{2} + 2Br^{-} \rightarrow Br_{2} + 2Cl^{-}  \)

Which of the following statements concerning the oxidising agent in this reaction is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: It loses electrons and is reduced.

options.label: B
options.text: It gains electrons and is reduced.

options.label: C
options.text: It loses electrons and is oxidised.

options.label: D
options.text: It gains electrons and is oxidised.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning ice and water is / are correct?

(1) Ice has an open structure but water does not.
(2) The number of hydrogen bonds formed between  \( H_{2}O \)  molecules in ice is double that in water.
(3) The shape of a  \( H_{2}O \)  molecule in ice is tetrahedral while the shape of a  \( H_{2}O \)  molecule in water is V-shaped.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
X and Z are two elements. The table below shows some physical properties of two bromides, XBr and ZBr.
Melting point / \( {}^{\circ} \) CBoiling point / \( {}^{\circ} \) CSolubility in water
XBr4591 325soluble
ZBr32105insoluble
Which of the following statements is / are correct? (1) XBr is a hard but brittle solid at room temperature. (2) ZBr has a giant covalent structure. (3) X is a metal and Z is a non-metal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
Members of the homologous series of alkenes have the same

(1) structural formula.
(2) empirical formula.
(3) general formula.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) The standard enthalpy change of formation of liquid nitrogen is not equal to zero.
(2) The standard enthalpy change of formation of a compound must be a negative value.
(3) The standard enthalpy change of formation of CO(g) can be determined directly from experiment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following set-ups can be used to prevent an underground iron pipe from rusting?
[IMG:0.jpg]

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following two solutions :
Solution A : 100 cm \( ^{3} \)  of 1.0 M nitric acid
Solution B : 100 cm \( ^{3} \)  of 0.5 M sulphuric acid

Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) Both solution A and solution B can react with iron.
(2) Complete neutralisation of solution A and complete neutralisation of solution B require the same volume of 1.0 M potassium hydroxide solution.
(3) When 0.05 mol of  \( CaCO_{3}(s) \)  is separately added to solution A and to solution B, the same number of moles of gas is evolved in both cases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning ethanoic acid are correct?

(1) Ethanoic acid is present in vinegar.
(2) Ethanoic acid changes phenolphthalein indicator from colourless to pink.
(3) Ethanoic acid is an alkanoic acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
1st statement : Butane and methylpropane have the same
physical properties.
2nd statement : Butane and methylpropane have the same
molecular formula.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In order to investigate the decomposition of \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \) to form \( O_{2}(g) \) , two experiments were carried out with \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \) in the presence of the same amount of a suitable catalyst at room conditions. The concentrations and volumes of \( H_{2}O_{2}(aq) \) used are shown below:
ExperimentConcentration of \( \mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})/\mathrm{M} \)Volume of \( \mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{2}(\mathrm{aq})/\text{cm}^{3} \)
I1.025
II0.550
Which of the following graphs correctly shows the variation in the volume of \( O_{2}(g) \) evolved with time in the above two experiments?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction :
\(  C_{3}H_{8}(g)+5O_{2}(g)\rightarrow3CO_{2}(g)+4H_{2}O(l) \)

If  \( 100 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( C_{3}H_{8}(g) \)  burns in  \( 600 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( O_{2}(g) \) , what would be the volume of the resulting gaseous mixture at room conditions?
(Molar volume of gas at room conditions = 24 dm \( ^{3} \) )

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following equilibrium system at room conditions :
\( \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{SCN}^{-}(\mathrm{aq})\rightleftharpoons\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{SCN})^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \)

A small amount of  \( \mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{NO}_{3})_{3}(\mathrm{s}) \)  is added to the equilibrium mixture, and a new equilibrium is finally attained at the same conditions. In comparison with the original equilibrium, which of the following combinations concerning this new equilibrium is correct?
Rate of backward reaction   |    Concentration of Fe(SCN) \( ^{2+} \) (aq)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following chemical reactions is the fastest at room conditions?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the information below :
Chemical reaction    |    Equilibrium constant at  \( 500^{\circ} \)C
HI(g)  \( \rightleftharpoons{} \)  \(\frac{1}{2}\)H \( _{2} \) (g) +  \( \frac{1}{2} \)I\( _{2} \) (g)    |    \( K_{1} \)
\( H_{2}(g) + I_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons{} \)  \(2HI(g)\)  |    \( K_{2} \)

Which of the following mathematical relationships concerning  \( K_{1} \)  and  \( K_{2} \)  is correct?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following pairs of substances would NOT react with each other?

options.label: C
options.text: propene and hydrogen in the presence of platinum

options.label: D
options.text: methylpropan-2-ol and acidified potassium dichromate solution

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
What is W in the following conversion?
[IMG:0.jpg]

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reversible reaction :
\( \mathbf{X}(\mathbf{a}\mathbf{q})\rightleftharpoons{}\mathbf{Y}(\mathbf{a}\mathbf{q}) \)

At a fixed temperature, the system initially contains a small amount of X(aq) only. When the system attains chemical equilibrium, the mole ratio of X to Y in the mixture is 1:3. Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) The reaction quotient  \( Q_{c} \)  of the system at any moment before equilibrium is greater than the equilibrium constant  \( K_{c} \)  for the reaction.
(2) If the experiment is repeated at the same temperature with the system initially containing a small amount of Y(aq) only, the mole ratio of X to Y in the equilibrium mixture is 1:3.
(3) At equilibrium, the rate of backward reaction is 3 times the rate of forward reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning the elements across the second period of the Periodic Table going from left to right is / are correct?

(1) The melting point of carbon is the highest among these elements.
(2) The electrical conductivity of lithium is the highest among these elements.
(3) The bond type of these elements changes from ionic bonding to covalent bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
An organic compound has the following structure :
[IMG:0.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning this compound is / are correct?

(1) It is soluble in water.
(2) It has an ester group.
(3) Its empirical formula is  \( CH_{2}O \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
P and Q are polyesters made from the same monomers. The average number of repeating units in P is 5 000 and that in Q is 80 000. Which of the following statements are correct?

(1) Q melts at a higher temperature than P.
(2) The average molecular mass of Q is greater than that of P.
(3) The number of carbon atoms in a repeating unit of Q is greater than that of P.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement : \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CH_{2}CH_{2}COO^{-}Na^{+} \)  is a soap.
2nd statement : \( CH_{3}CH_{2}CH_{2}CH_{2}COOH^{-}Na^{+} \)  contains a hydrophilic \(-COO^{-}\) group.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
